title: Malmesbury: Burton Hill Family Weekend

main_route.excerpt: a big lunch in the Jim Kirbys & David Kirby - 14 of us & Tonic. Burton Hill.

geocode_hint.hint: Burton Hill is a hamlet immediately south of Malmesbury, Wiltshire; Jim Kirby and David Kirby hosted the family there; Malmesbury Abbey visited on foot the same afternoon

main_route.excerpt: Straight after breakfast, John, Maia, Charles & Dylan asked to walk to the play ground with Tonic

stops.excerpt: In the afternoon we went on a beautiful long walk in & around Malmesbury. The Abbey is gorgeous – Romanesque arches & carvings. A superb entry porch, & the Tomb of the first king. The tombstone of a woman eaten by a tiger.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page A_0219_R contains the August 22-23 Beauchamp/Gus King entries which belong to a separate trip (1997-beauchamp-peter-s-quartet-gus-king) already extracted; this trip begins with the 'Saturday September 6th' heading.
mixed_end: page A_0220_R is boundary-mixed; it shows a postcard of Malmesbury Abbey South Porch carvings and belongs to this trip's context, but the diary text ends on A_0220_L.
The 'Saturday September 6th' entry records listening to Princess Diana's funeral on the radio while driving to Malmesbury, confirming the date as 6 September 1997 (Diana's funeral was held that day).

14 people at lunch including Jim Kirbys and David Kirby; exact family members present beyond John and Charles (named on Sunday) are not individually listed; Thomas, William, and Peter may have attended given the extended family gathering but cannot be confirmed from the text.
Return to Birmingham is not explicitly narrated; the diary ends on Sunday morning at Burton Hill. Return home is inferred as standard trip conclusion.
summary: On Saturday 6 September 1997, the family drove from Birmingham to Burton Hill near Malmesbury, Wiltshire, listening to Princess Diana's funeral on the radio. They joined a large family gathering of 14 people at the Kirby family home, visited Malmesbury Abbey on a long afternoon walk, and stayed overnight. On Sunday morning the children walked to a local playground before the family returned home.
extraction_notes: Pages A_0219_R and A_0220_L-R. The spread is boundary-mixed: A_0219_R contains three separate diary entries (pre-existing trip tail, Aug 22-23 Beauchamp trip, and this Sep 6 entry); the Aug entries are captured under trip_id 1997-beauchamp-peter-s-quartet-gus-king. This trip covers only the 'Saturday September 6th' and 'Sunday Sept 7th' entries. Burton Hill is identified as a hamlet adjacent to Malmesbury in Wiltshire; the Kirby family hosted the gathering there. The Malmesbury Abbey walk was an afternoon excursion from the Burton Hill base and not a separate overnight stop. A_0220_R is a postcard page with three photographs of Malmesbury Abbey South Porch stonework, confirming the visit. No crossings; entirely domestic England trip. Diana's funeral broadcast (6 September 1997) corroborates the diary date.
